1760:39 let's talk about everyone's favorite topic which is called the H or the higher order component so what are these
1760:46 and why should youone to care about there so the higher order component or H design pattern is used to reuse
1760:52 component logic and it is a function that takes a component as argument and return a new component that enhances the
1760:59 original one these patterns allows us to share functionalities across multip component without repeating our code so
1761:06 that was just a quick definition of what H is and now let me give you a quick note about the H so an hocc or higher
1761:13 order component does not modify the original component directly but instead
1761:19 wraps it in a new component thereby adding additional Behavior or modifying its props so yeah that was just a quick
1761:26 note and now let me give you a quick example of how our regular component looks like and how our higher order comp

1871:04 it involves splitting a complex component into smaller individual subcomponent that can be used together

1871:16 compound component is but now here is a bit note about the compound component this pattern is useful when you want to
1871:22 create a component that has multiple sub components but you want to maintain the ability to customize or control the
1871:30 behavior of each subc component from the parent or the container let's suppose if you want to build this card without

1906:57 is called the render prop so the render prop design pattern is used to share code between components by passing a
1907:03 function as a PRP to a component so this function which is typically referred to as a render prop return a react element
1907:11 that gets rendered so this pattern allow a component to be more flexible and reusable as the parent component can
1907:17 control the rendering of the content in its child component so you know what let me just give you example of there here

1993:48 just break them down one by one the first one we have is a unit testing and this course is totally focused on unit
1993:55 testing so what is a unit testing and why should everyone care about that so unit testing is where developer test
1994:01 individual component of an application like functions or classes to ensure
1994:06 their work is expected and these test focus on small isolated part of your
1994:12 code okay so that was about the unit testing and in this course we're going to be covering a unit testing so next
1994:18 one we have is a integration testing so integration testing or integration testing is about checking how different
1994:25 part of your application works together while unit test focus on individual component which we just saw a few
1994:32 seconds ago integration testing ensure that module database apis and other
1994:37 services interact correctly so that's just the integration testing and we're not going to be covering that in this
1994:43 course and finally we have end to end testing so end to end testing simulates real user scenarios to make sure the

1994:55 system including the front end and also the back end if you are aware of those topics and any external Services which
1995:02 we're not going to be covering like any services and that kind of stuff to ensure everything integrates and

2022:42 pattern in testings so what do I mean by AAA a a mean arrange a mean act a mean
2022:49 assert so how that's going to look like this is how it's going to look like so the first step that we have is a arrange
2022:54 and in this step we set up everything needed for the test like variables mock data and Etc you don't know where a mock
2023:02 data is but we are going to be covering that in a lot of great detail in this course then the next thing that we have
2023:07 is a act so act perform the action that you want to test like for example calling a function or doing something
2023:15 and the final step that we have is assert so it verifies that the result of the action is what you expect so you
2023:22 know let me just put the in more simple word so imagine you are baking a cake so in the arrange part you're going to be
2023:29 gathering all the ingredients and tools you need like for example the floor sugar eggs mixing bow Etc and in the egg
2023:36 phase you're going to be mixing those ingredients together and putting the cake in the on and then finally we have
2023:43 the assert so in this phase we are going to be checking if the cake is baked properly by cutting it and then testing
2023:49 it so if the test good and is fully bed you know your steps work so that was
2023:54 just a quick analogy about the a AA so now let me just explain that in the coring so range is when you gather
2023:60 everything you need for the test and act is when you do the action you are testing like calling a function or doing
2024:05 something and then assert is when you check if the result is what you expected like testing the cake to see if it's

2063:12 everyone care about there so TTD or test development is a way of writing your code where you first write a test for
2063:19 your code then write the code to make the test pass and finally refactor your code while keeping your test passing I
2063:26 know there was a lot of gibberish but now let me just break this down for you so how that's going to look like first
2063:31 of all you're going to be writing your test so think about what you want your code to do and then write a test for
2063:37 your code after then run your test but in this case your test will fail because you don't even have your actual code so
2063:44 your test will fail after that you would need to write some code so just write enough code so it will make your test
2063:51 passed after that you have to run your test again and in this case your test should pass because your code works as
2063:58 expected then you have to refactor your code so clean up your code if needed and make sure your test still passed after
2064:05 that repeat that process again and again and this is what we call a tdd if you still don't get it so here is the final
2064:13 definition you would first of all write your test then you would write your code then you would make your test pass

2082:31 so it will check if the system behaves as expected when provided with the valid inputs so in positive testing you are
2082:37 testing the system with correct or valid inputs ensuring that the system function as expected when used correctly so on
2082:44 the other hand we have a negative testing so it will check if the system handles invalid or unexpected inputs
2082:51 correctly in negative testing you are providing an incorrect or invalid inputs to ensure that the system can handle
2082:57 error scenarios gracefully and it doesn't crash or behaves unexpectedly so
2083:02 that was just the definition of a positive and negative testing so how that's going to look like first of all

2087:05 primary idea behind the boundary testing is that errors are more likely to occur in the boundaries in the extreme end of
2087:12 the input ranges by testing the boundaries of an input values you can find effects that occur when the system
2087:18 handles these hge cases I know that was a crazy definition of boundary testing

2087:30 minimum boundary which will test the smallest possible valid value then we have a maximum boundary which will test
2087:37 the largest possible valid value then we have a just in inside the boundary and
2087:42 it will test above the minimum or just below the maximum then we have a just outside the boundary so it will test
2087:49 just below the minimum or just above the maximum to ensure the invalid values are
2087:54 correctly rejected so that was just a types of um boundary testing but now let

2230:23 library from the testing Library so what is a design system there is no actual
2230:29 definition to where a design system is and why is that that's because if you ask a designer they will say it's a UI
2230:35 kit or it's a color palette or typography Grid or iconography but if you ask a developer they will say it's a
2230:42 component Library style guides or some sort of a documentation but the truth is a design system is all of there a little
2230:49 design and a little engineering so what do I mean by that well according to google a design system is a
2230:55 comprehensive set of standard guidelines and components that ensure consistency and cohesion in the design and
2231:01 development of a digital product it serve as a shared language and resource for design teams developers and other
2231:07 stakeholders to create a cohesive user experience across various platforms and products so that was a bit weird
2231:14 definition of what a design system is but now to put that as simple as possible you can think about the design
2231:20 system is like a toolkit for building a digital products it includes everything you need to make sure the design is
2231:26 consistent and looks the same across different screens or Pages now let's talk about the basic comp components of
2231:32 a design system the first one we have is a design principle and it's a high level guidelines or philosophies that inform
2231:38 design decisions like Simplicity accessibility or usability the second one is a UI component and they are a
2231:45 reusable element such as buttons forms model navigation bar and other common interface element often designed with
2231:52 responsiveness and accessibility in mind third one we have is a patterns and they are repeated design solution to common
2231:58 problems such as card layout grade or r down menus and so on next we have is a
2232:04 style guide and there are a collection of visual design rules such as typography uh color pallet and there are
2232:10 a lot of them which we are going to be covering in this course next we have a branding they ensure the design system
2232:15 aligns with the company brand identity tone and value then we have a documentation and they are a clean
2232:22 instruction and best practices on how to use a component and pattern correctly as well as how to maintain the system
2232:28 overall time and then we have a code which is every everyone's favorite topic so for technical consistencies this
2232:35 includes frontend code like component or UI framework such as react components or css Frameworks that developer can
2232:42 integrate into their application something which we are going to be building by ourselves the final piece of the puzzle we have is accessibility and
2232:49 their recommendation for creating a product they are usable by people with disabilities ensuring that the design
2232:54 system support complains with accessibility standards as well so now let me give you a practical example of a
2232:59 design system so let's say you are building bu a shopping app for a brand and you want everything like buttons
2233:05 colors fonts iconography layout and so much more to look consistent across the
2233:10 app a design system would help you achieve this so how that's going to look like well in your design system you

2262:00 let me just give you a definition of what a story book is so story book is an open source tool used for developing and
2262:06 testing the UI components in isolation it allows developer to create a UI components outside of their main
2262:13 application making it easier to build test and showcase individual part of the UI before they are integrated into your
2262:19 overall application so as I said this is not a tool that you are going to be using like day to-day and also this is
2262:26 another a tool that uh you are going to be using there for a smaller application so it provides a lot of benefits the
2262:32 first thing it provides is a component isolation so story book Let's developer work on the UI component in isolation
2262:39 meaning you can build and test component without the need to run the entire application and this helps in focusing
2262:46 purely in the individual component functionality rather than just focusing on the entire application it also
2262:52 provides something called the visual testing so it provides a visual interface to view different state of the UI component such as various
2262:58 configuration themes or data inputs and so on and so forth something which we are going to be covering in this course
2263:04 so this helps identify issues with the component visual appearance or behavior early in the development process it also
2263:11 provides the documentation and I've seen a lot of developers they use story book just for their documentation not for
2263:17 anything else but just for the documentation so story books serve as a living documentation tool for the UI
2263:23 component you can write stories like individual use cases for each component and we are going to be also covering the
2263:28 stories in later in this course and they are automatically display in the story book interface something which you're
2263:34 going to be seeing in a few seconds it's easy to maintain and update documentation is the component evolves
2263:39 it also provide the support of add-ons or you can say the extensions so story
2263:45 book has a wide array of add-ons that can help with task like accessibility checks responsive design testing or
2263:51 adding a control to multiple components St dynamically something which we're going to be also covering in this course

2331:47 and also about the addons inside the story book so what is a style dictionary and why should anyone care about that so
2331:53 style dictionary is a build system and tool that allows designers and developers to Define organize and manage
2331:60 design tokens in a Json format so these tokens Can Be mapped to different formats such as CSS variables sense
2332:07 variables or even JavaScript objects so what are the benefits of using a style dictionary well the F benefit is a
2332:13 centralized management so design tokens are stored in a single source of Truth typically in Json file making them easy
2332:20 to maintain and update it is also a crossplatform it can output design tokens in different formats like CSS
2332:26 Json SS L and and even Android iOS specific formats extensibility you can
2332:32 extend and customize how tokens are processed enabling you to create complex design system with calculated values
2332:38 custom Transformations and so much more which we are going to be covering in a short period of time and finally
2332:44 automation so style dictionary automates the process of transforming design tokens into usable formats for the
2332:50 development so how all of this going to work so first of all you would have our input. Json file and inside there we
2332:56 would have our design token and once we use a style dictionary on there so it's going to gives us the output like so in
2333:03 this case we are now using a CSS variables but if you want to get like a JavaScript version of there or a SS
